body.label: group_1.root.thm
body.claim: A group G satisfying \((a*b)^2 = a^2*b^2\) for all \(a,b\in G\) is Abelian.

proof_steps.variable_type: a group such that for all \(a,b\in G\), \((ab)^2=a^2b^2\)
proof_steps.statement: Let \(G\) be a group such that for all \(a,b\in G\), \((ab)^2=a^2b^2\).

proof_steps.proof_method: Expand the squares in the given identity using the definition of powers/products in a group.
Specialize the expanded identity to the fixed arbitrary elements \(a,b\).

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If \(x=y\) in a group, then \(gx=gy\) for any group element \(g\).
deduced_from_theorem.name: compatibility of equality with left multiplication

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A group operation is associative: \((xy)z=x(yz)\) for all group elements.
deduced_from_theorem.name: associativity
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to reassociate the products after multiplying by \(a^{-1}\).

proof_steps.proof_method: Multiply both sides of \(abab=aabb\) on the left by \(a^{-1}\), using associativity.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A group operation is associative: \((xy)z=x(yz)\) for all group elements.
deduced_from_theorem.name: associativity
left identity law
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to remove the leading identity element from both sides.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If \(x=y\) in a group, then \(xh=yh\) for any group element \(h\).
deduced_from_theorem.name: compatibility of equality with right multiplication

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A group operation is associative: \((xy)z=x(yz)\) for all group elements.
deduced_from_theorem.name: associativity
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to reassociate the products after multiplying by \(b^{-1}\).

proof_steps.proof_method: Multiply both sides on the right by \(b^{-1}\), using associativity.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A group operation is associative: \((xy)z=x(yz)\) for all group elements.
deduced_from_theorem.name: associativity
right identity law
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to remove the trailing identity element from both sides.

proof_steps.proof_method: Simplify using the right identity law.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If an assertion holds for arbitrary elements of a set, then it holds for all elements of that set.
deduced_from_theorem.name: universal generalization
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to conclude the equality for all pairs of elements.

proof_steps.proof_method: Since \(a\) and \(b\) were arbitrary, generalize the equality to all elements of \(G\).

deduced_from_theorem.claim: A group is Abelian if and only if its operation is commutative, i.e. \(xy=yx\) for all elements \(x,y\) of the group.
deduced_from_theorem.name: definition of Abelian group
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to identify commutativity as the Abelian property.

proof_steps.proof_method: Apply the definition of an Abelian group.

proof.text:
Let $G$ be a group such that for all $a,b \in G$ we have
\[
(ab)^2 = a^2 b^2.
\]
By the definition of the group operation, this identity means
\[
abab = aabb
\]
for all $a,b \in G$.

Fix arbitrary elements $a,b \in G$. We want to prove that $ab = ba$. Since $abab = aabb$, we can manipulate this equality using the group axioms.

First, multiply both sides on the left by $a^{-1}$. Using associativity of the group operation, this gives
\[
a^{-1}(abab) = a^{-1}(aabb).
\]
Rewriting each side using associativity:
\[
(a^{-1}a)bab = (a^{-1}a)abb.
\]
Since $a^{-1}a = e$, where $e$ is the identity element of $G$, this simplifies to
\[
ebab = eabb,
\]
so
\[
bab = abb.
\]

Next, multiply both sides of this new equality on the right by $b^{-1}$. Using associativity again, we obtain
\[
(bab)b^{-1} = (abb)b^{-1}.
\]
Rewriting each side:
\[
ba(bb^{-1}) = ab(b b^{-1}).
\]
Since $bb^{-1} = e$, this becomes
\[
ba e = ab e.
\]
Because $xe = x$ for all $x \in G$, we have
\[
ba = ab.
\]

Since $a$ and $b$ were arbitrary elements of $G$, this shows that $ab = ba$ for all $a,b \in G$. Therefore $G$ is Abelian.
